name: Web Portals Conformance Demo
summary: The runnable proof of the Web Portals runtime contract (#1001) in a real browser: a deeply nested component portals a modal to a top-level outlet that escapes its clipped, transformed stacking context, while CONTEXT preservation (resolved via logical ancestry, not the physical DOM) and LOGICAL event bubbling (a click inside the portal firing a handler on the logical ancestor via composedLogical) both verifiably hold. Exercises the actual webportals plug (plugs/webportals/) in the browser — the directive projects the modal into the outlet, logicalParent links the projection to its declaration site, and dispatchLogical walks the logical chain. A runtime-conformance section asserts each invariant live: the modal relocates into the outlet and escapes the clip, its logicalParent is the declaration site, context resolves via logical ancestry but NOT via the physical DOM ancestry, and a click inside the portal bubbles to a handler on the logical ancestor.
description:
Concept

The runnable proof of the Web Portals runtime contract (#1001) in a real browser. A deeply nested component portals a modal to a top-level outlet that escapes its stacking context, while context preservation and logical event bubbling both hold.

The actual plug, in the browser

This exercises the real webportals plug (plugs/webportals/): the directive projects the modal into the outlet, logicalParent links the projection to its declaration site, and dispatchLogical walks the logical chain. It doubles as the platform-first forcing function for #1148/#1149/#1150.

Verified contract

The runtime-conformance section asserts each invariant live: the modal relocates into the outlet and escapes the clip, its logicalParent is the declaration site, context resolves via logical ancestry but not via the physical DOM ancestry, and a click inside the portal bubbles to a handler on the logical ancestor via composedLogical.
